2026-04-16T02:07:47.8757950Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8758554Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8758993Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8759547Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8760319Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8760588Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8760748Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8760929Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8761106Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8761338Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8761543Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8761838Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:07:47.8762117Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-16T02:11:13.2777084Z **Summary of gaps found in `Azure.Communication.Messages` (PR #56911 by @MaiLinhP):**

2026-04-16T02:11:20.1847026Z ◆ Let me check the README to understand what's documented and look at the API changes to identify gaps. The README has basic examples for:
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1848236Z   1. NotificationMessagesClient - send text, template, media messages
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1848911Z   2. MessageTemplateClient - get templates
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1849312Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1851186Z   From the commit, the new API includes ConversationAdministrationClient, ConversationThreadClient, and various new message content types (reaction, sticker, interactive, etc.) added in 1.2.0-beta.1, 1.3.0-beta.1, 1.3.0-beta.2.
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1853032Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1853307Z   Looking at the README:
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1853989Z   - The Key Concepts section is very brief and only mentions NotificationMessagesClient
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1854721Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1854995Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1855689Z   - ConversationAdministrationClient and ConversationThreadClient aren't documented at all
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1857184Z   - Unified Messaging, Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message types from the beta releases have no coverage
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1858527Z   - The README doesn't address WhatsApp-specific types or the newer message content options
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1859296Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1860704Z   But I need to focus on what actually changed in the latest commit - the Management Generator fix #58153 - which appears to be an internal generator change without direct API surface changes.
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1862066Z   
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1868287Z   The more significant documentation gap comes from the Azure.Communication.Messages migration in commit #56911. The Key Concepts section only covers NotificationMessagesClient and skips the other clients entirely, there's no working example for Microsoft Entra ID authentication despite recommending it for production, and the newer capabilities from the beta releases aren't documented in the README or changelog. The CHANGELOG entry for 1.3.0-beta.3 is empty despite the generator migration happening, and the README has significant gaps—it's missing documentation for the Conversation clients added in earlier betas, interactive messages and reactions from 1.2.0-beta.1, and the Entra ID authentication example. I should file an issue capturing these documentation gaps.
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1873531Z

2026-04-16T02:11:20.1877317Z   1. `ConversationAdministrationClient` - for managing conversations (Unified Messaging, added in 1.3.0-beta.1)
2026-04-16T02:11:20.1878032Z   2. `ConversationThreadClient` - for messaging in conversation threads (added in 1.3.0-beta.1)

2026-04-16T02:11:20.1879246Z   4. Read receipt and typing indicator (added in 1.3.0-beta.2)
